title: event-study output
description: Canonical structured output for the event-study skill. Layer 1 (the data); Layer 2 (rendered note/table/summary) is described in references/rendering.md. The shape is discriminated by `mode`: single, cross_section, or aggregate. All three carry the same per-subject blocks; the difference is whether the rendering layer renders one subject (single), a row per subject (cross_section), or only the aggregate (aggregate).

mode.description: Discriminant. Picks the rendered output shape. single = 1 ticker, 1 event; cross_section = N tickers, 1 event period; aggregate = N tickers, all events in window.

tier.description: Data fidelity for the event class. A = Benzinga earnings (true print date + surprise %); B = stocks-only fallback (8-K acceptance date, no surprise). Tier is meaningful only for the earnings class; dividend_changes and large_volume_spike are tier-agnostic and report A.

tier_caveats.description: Human-readable caveats when tier < A. Empty array on Tier A.

model.description: Benchmark model used to compute abnormal returns. v1 supports `spy` only (AR = raw - SPY); v2 will add `capm` (per-name beta). Reserved here so consumers don't break when capm ships.

take.description: One-sentence trade-relevant conclusion. Surfaced from the strongest signal across the analysis (regime shift, significant t-stat, strong surprise-vs-reaction correlation).

subjects.description: One entry per (ticker, event) pair. In single mode this has exactly 1 entry. In cross_section it has N entries (1 per ticker for the chosen period). In aggregate it has N*K entries (all matching events across all tickers).

event_date.description: T0 calendar date. For AMC events, T0 is the date of the press release (returns are measured from that day's close); the close used at T0 is the close on the day BEFORE if the event was BMO (so T+1 captures the open-day reaction).

event_session.description: Event timing relative to market hours. Drives which trading day is T0 in the return computation.

event_metadata.description: Class-specific metadata. For earnings: fiscal_period, fiscal_year, surprise_eps_pct, estimated_eps. For dividend_changes: prior_amount, new_amount, change_pct. For large_volume_spike: volume, trailing_30d_avg, z_score.

event_window_returns.description: Raw and SPY returns at each horizon, anchored at T0 close. T0 close is the ticker's close on the trading day BEFORE T+1 (i.e. the last close that did NOT yet reflect the event). For AMC events the trading day of the press release is T0; for BMO events the trading day BEFORE is T0.

abnormal_returns.description: Per-horizon abnormal return = raw - spy. CAR is the cumulative abnormal return from T+1 through the horizon (for a daily model with non-compounded sum, CAR(T+5) == AR(T+5) here; the schema names the field CAR for forward compatibility with multi-day-window event types).

car_t5_pct.description: Cumulative abnormal return through T+5. For the SPY model with daily close-to-close measurement this equals ar_t5_pct; preserved as its own field so a v2 multi-day window doesn't break consumers.

t_stat_vs_history.description: For this name, compare this event's T+5 CAR to the distribution of prior events of the same class. Null when the name has <8 prior events (sample too small for the t-test to be reliable; the rendered output still shows the raw comparison but marks it 'underpowered').

z_score.description: (this_event_t5_car - prior_mean) / prior_std. Reported even at small n; the `underpowered` flag warns the reader.

percentile.description: Percentile of this event within the prior distribution (0.0 to 1.0).

underpowered.description: True when prior_n < 8. The number is still emitted so the reader can judge; the flag prevents action on a 4-observation distribution.

direction_concurrence.description: For earnings class only: 'X/N' count of prior events where the sign of the abnormal return matched the sign of the surprise. e.g. '5/8'. Null for classes without a direction signal.

summary.description: Cross-sectional and aggregate statistics. Null in single mode. Populated in cross_section and aggregate.

t_stat_avg_vs_zero.description: mean / (std / sqrt(n)). Tests whether the average CAR across events is reliably non-zero.

horizon_breakdown.description: Aggregate stats at each horizon (T+1, T+3, T+5). Used in aggregate mode rendering.

percentiles.description: Distribution percentiles of T+5 CARs across the cross-section.

surprise_reaction_correlation.description: For earnings class on Tier A only: Pearson correlation between surprise_eps_pct and ar_t5_pct across the cross-section. Null for other classes or Tier B (no surprise data).

regime_check.description: Only populated in aggregate mode when n_subjects >= 8. Compares the most-recent 4 events' avg CAR to the full window mean.

regime_shift_flag.description: True when |recent_mean - full_mean| > 1 standard error of the full mean.

distribution_shape.description: KDE-derived shape summary of the T+5 CAR distribution. Only populated when n_subjects >= 10. Surfaces bimodality and fat tails that mean/median/t-stat hide.

x.description: CAR value in decimal (e.g. 0.028 = +2.8%) where the KDE has a prominent local maximum.

sparkline.description: 40-char UTF-8 block sparkline of the KDE density on a uniform grid across [min(sample) - 0.5h, max(sample) + 0.5h].

window.description: The query window. Populated in cross_section (single period) and aggregate (date range).

period_label.description: User-supplied label like 'Q1 2026' or 'most-recent-quarter'. Cross-section only.
